channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump has 'absolute' immunity only for official acts, sends case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: READ: Supreme Court ruling in Trump immunity case
item.description: The Supreme Court’s ruling comes less than five months before Election Day, when Trump hopes to retake the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court dismisses state challenges to red state restrictions on social media platforms
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday weighed in on a Florida law that restrict how large social media companies moderate user content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump immunity case: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have substantial protection from prosecution
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed in on whether a former president has immunity from prosecution for conduct allegedly involving official acts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court sends Trump immunity case back to lower court, dimming chance of trial before election
item.description: The Supreme Court has extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects he could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden campaign says Biden's fundraising cash would go to Kamala Harris if he drops out as top donors waver
item.description: President Biden's campaign is fending off questions from donors after his disastrous debate performance last week, with some even asking for refunds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden family blame staff for debate performance as he lays low again at Camp David: reports
item.description: President Biden is laying low at Camp David and won't return to White House until Monday night after his family huddled at the Maryland residence blamed staffers for the debate flop.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Summer EBT program will help struggling NY families
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York State began accepting applications Monday for a new program designed to help families with school-aged children buy groceries this summer. Families in the Summer EBT program will receive a one-time installment of $120 per child for the summer.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Gathered at Camp David, Biden's family tells him to stay in the presidential race and keep fighting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden's family used a gathering at Camp David to urge him to stay in the race and keep fighting despite his dreadful debate performance, and some members criticized how his staff prepared him for the faceoff, according to four people familiar with the discussions.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan governor warned Biden campaign her state is no longer winnable: report
item.description: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer reportedly warned President Biden's campaign that Michigan is no longer winnable following Biden's debate performance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayor Adams on how $112.4 billion budget will shape future of NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Monday is the first day of the new fiscal year for New York City and the ink is still drying on the record-breaking $112.4 billion budget deal signed by the City Council.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Some student loan borrowers have July payment waived: Here's why
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of student loan borrowers will not have to make debt payments this month. About 3 million borrowers will have both their July payment and interest charges waived.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Texas opens more coastal waters for carbon storage wells
item.description: The Gulf Coast is poised to be a major hub for underground disposal of greenhouse gas to mitigate climate change, but questions remain over who will fund it long-term.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Dylan Baddour, Inside Climate News, KPRC 2

item.title: Republican candidates are downplaying abortion, but it keeps coming up
item.description: Torn between a base that wants more restrictions on abortion and a moderate majority that does not, it seems many Republicans would rather avoid the topic. But they can’t escape talking about it.
item.dc:creator: Julie Rovner, MPR News

item.title: The Republicans who want to be Trump's VP were once harsh critics with key policy differences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s hard to refer to someone as “Hitler” and end up in their good graces, let alone potentially become the person they choose to help lead the country. But Ohio Sen.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fetterman on Dems abandoning Biden: 'Dumbest s--- I've ever heard'
item.description: “And that that whole abandon Biden thing, it's the — that's the dumbest s--- I’ve ever heard,” Sen. John Fetterman said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sforza, NewsNation

item.title: Biden speculation swirls as White House, allies fight off calls to drop out
item.description: The White House and Biden himself at fundraisers and on social media said the president had no intention of dropping out of the race.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: How will Louisiana's new Ten Commandments classroom requirement be funded and enforced?
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Even as a legal challenge is already underway over a new Louisiana law requiring the Ten Commandments to be displayed in classrooms, the details of how the mandate will be implemented and enforced remain murky.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's why it would be tough for Democrats to replace Joe Biden on the presidential ticket
item.description: It would be nearly impossible for Democrats to replace President Joe Biden as their 2024 presidential nominee unless he chooses to step aside following his halting debate performance against ex-President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A private call of top Democrats fuels more insider anger about Biden's debate performance
item.description: A sense of deep concern is growing among some Democratic officials that leaders inside Joe Biden’s campaign and the Democratic National Committee are not taking seriously enough the impact of the president’s troubling debate performance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's VP contenders jockey for primacy after first debate
item.description: Donald Trump’s top vice presidential contenders are using his contentious first debate with President Biden to jockey for primacy as the former president prepares to pick his running mate. Vice presidential prospects like Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.), North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum (R) and Sen.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Tim Scott has benefited from mentors along the way. He's hoping for another helping hand
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When Tim Scott was a teenager, a Chick-fil-A manager named John Moniz offered him a sandwich, a job, and four years of indispensable mentoring about how to be a businessman and a citizen.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's difficult debate puts spotlight on Harris
item.description: President Biden’s lackluster debate performance has thrust Vice President Harris into the spotlight. After Biden struggled against former President Trump, Harris hit the airwaves to defend her running mate’s record and ability to handle the job.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: George Latimer sets his sights on the NY-16 general election
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Westchester County Executive George Latimer defeated incumbent Rep. Jamaal Bowman with 58 percent of the vote on Tuesday in the NY-16 Democratic primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Democrats fret about hit to Senate chances after Biden debate debacle
item.description: Democrats are worried President Biden's dismal debate performance could doom any slim chance the party has for holding its Senate majority by further depressing turnout in critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans are turning Biden’s voter registration order into a partisan flash point
item.description: Three years after President Biden issued an executive order for boosting voter registration, GOP officials are ramping up efforts to turn it into a partisan flash point before this fall’s election.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Biden to discuss future of re-election campaign with family on Sunday
item.description: Biden’s trip to Camp David was planned before Thursday’s debate. He and first lady Jill Biden are scheduled to join their children and grandchildren there late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Carol E. Lee, Kristen Welker, Jonathan Allen, Mike Memoli and Monica Alba, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Poll: Could Gavin Newsom beat Donald Trump?
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom, acting as a surrogate for President Biden following Thursday night's debate, once again deflected questions over whether he might potentially replace Biden on the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: How replacing Biden as Democratic nominee would actually work
item.description: Replacing President Biden as the Democratic nominee would be highly complicated, and likely impossible, unless the president voluntarily decided to back out on his own.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court obstruction ruling impacts Tampa Bay Capitol rioters
item.description: The Tampa Bay area has the most Jan. 6 rioters charged per capita in the United States, and some may be impacted by Friday's U.S. Supreme Court ruling about an obstruction-related charge.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 49 percent say Democrats should nominate someone other than Biden: Survey
item.description: Close to half of Americans believe the Democratic Party should replace President Biden in the 2024 election, per a YouGov poll conducted the morning after the first presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, Fox 5

item.title: CNN poll: Most viewers say Trump won debate
item.description: A CNN poll of those who watched the debate late Thursday found 67 percent said former President Donald Trump won the debate, compared to 33 percent who said President Joe Biden won.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Did Trump actually call veterans who died in wars 'suckers'?
item.description: Near the end of the first 30 minutes, Biden claimed Trump previously verbally attacked and disrespected American veterans, particularly ones who were injured or died during combat.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways on a pivotal Trump-Biden debate
item.description: President Biden and former President Trump met for the first time in four years on a debate stage in Atlanta on Thursday in what was cast as a pivotal moment in the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Biden, Trump take shots during brief immigration debate
item.description: Despite much hype that the border and immigration were expected to feature prominently in the first U.S. presidential debate between President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump on Thursday night, the topics were barely discussed and with little substance.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 memorable moments from the heated Biden-Trump debate
item.description: The two men tried to make their appeals to voters as concerns swirled over both of them. But it was Biden's faltering answers in the first 30 minutes of the debate that largely overshadowed everything else.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, Fox 5

item.title: Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont tests positive for COVID-19
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (PIX11) -- Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont has tested positive for COVID-19, a spokesperson said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
